intro: The pivotal question between OpenX and Teads is how much of your setup stays portable: OpenX carries low lock-in and Teads carries high lock-in. OpenX is programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers. Teads is outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers. Pick OpenX when the job is large publishers wanting another demand source in their header bidding wrapper and you accept low lock-in; pick Teads when it is content publishers monetizing mobile web video with brand-safe premium demand and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows OpenX at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience, and Teads at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — OpenX is a poor fit for mobile-only app publishers needing sdk-first tooling and app-specific demand, and Teads is a poor fit for mobile game developers seeking performance-driven rewarded or interstitial fill. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: OpenX is for programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform; Teads is for outstream video ad marketplace; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose OpenX if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work maps to programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers.
verdict_b: Choose Teads if you can tolerate some setup friction and the work lines up with outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers.

faq.a: Neither OpenX nor Teads publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — OpenX uses a custom model and Teads uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from OpenX to Teads?
faq.a: Moving from OpenX to Teads is feasible, but Teads is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both OpenX and Teads rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Teads a good alternative to OpenX?
faq.a: Yes — Teads is a reasonable alternative to OpenX for large publishers wanting another demand source in their header bidding wrapper.. The practical differences are custom-vs-custom billing and low-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Teads as a credible swap.
